dna_damage.interpretation: Elevated radiation exposure and long mission duration may increase predicted DNA damage response activity.
dna_damage.why_it_matters: DNA damage response pathways are important for genome stability, radiation biology, and long-term cancer risk monitoring.
dna_damage.nasa_connection: NASA GeneLab and space biology resources discuss space radiation as a driver of DNA damage and stress-response biology.
dna_damage.evidence_source: NASA GeneLab / OSDR space radiation and omics studies

mitochondrial_stress.interpretation: Spaceflight-related stressors may disrupt mitochondrial energy production and increase oxidative stress.
mitochondrial_stress.why_it_matters: A major multi-omics spaceflight analysis identified mitochondrial stress as a central biological hub of spaceflight impact.
mitochondrial_stress.nasa_connection: OSD-343 is associated with the study 'Comprehensive multi-omics analysis reveals mitochondrial stress as a central biological hub for spaceflight impact.'
mitochondrial_stress.evidence_source: NASA OSDR OSD-343 / Cell 2020 multi-omics spaceflight analysis

immune_dysfunction.interpretation: Long-duration spaceflight may alter immune regulation, especially under radiation, stress, and altered physiological conditions.
immune_dysfunction.why_it_matters: Immune dysregulation is relevant to infection response, inflammation, recovery from damage, and astronaut health monitoring.
immune_dysfunction.nasa_connection: Spaceflight and analog studies have reported altered immune response and inflammatory signaling under space-relevant stressors.
immune_dysfunction.evidence_source: NASA GeneLab / OSDR immune and spaceflight stress studies

inflammation.interpretation: Radiation and prolonged environmental stress may increase inflammatory signaling.
inflammation.why_it_matters: Chronic inflammation is connected to immune imbalance, tissue stress, aging biology, and disease risk.
inflammation.nasa_connection: Space radiation and microgravity-related studies have linked space-relevant stressors to cytokine and inflammatory responses.
inflammation.evidence_source: NASA GeneLab / OSDR radiation and inflammation studies

senescence.interpretation: Long missions with elevated radiation may increase predicted aging-associated cellular stress.
senescence.why_it_matters: Senescence overlaps with aging, inflammation, tissue dysfunction, and cancer biology.
senescence.nasa_connection: Spaceflight biology studies connect radiation, mitochondrial stress, DNA damage, and aging-associated pathway changes.
senescence.evidence_source: NASA OSDR / GeneLab aging and mitochondrial stress studies
